title: Merrill's Garden
content:
В В В В There is a garden where the seeded stems of thin long grass are bowed
В В В В Beneath July's slow rains and heat and tired children's trailing feet;
В В В В And the trees' neglected branches droop and make a cloud beneath the cloud,
В В В В And in that dark the crimson dew of raspberries shines more sweet than sweet.

В В В В The flower of the tall acacia's gone, the acacia's flower is white no more,
В В В В The aspen lifts his pithless arms, the aspen leaves are close and still;
В В В В The wind that tossed the clouds along, gray clouds and white like feathers bore,
В В В В Lets even a feather faintly fall and smoke spread hugely where it will.

В В В В But though the acacia's flower is gone and raspberries bear bright fruit untasted,
В В В В Beauty lives there, oh rich and rare, past the sum of eager June.
В В В В The lime tree's pyramid of flower and leaf and yellow flower unwasted
В В В В Rises at eve and bars the breast wild-heaving of the timid moon.

В В В В Now the tall pear-trees unrebuked lift their green fingers to the sky;
В В В В Their lower boughs are crossed like arms of templars in long stony sleep.
В В В В Their arms are crossed as though the wind, returning from wild war on high,
В В В В Had touched them with an angry breath, or whispered from his cavern deep.

В В В В A foxglove lifts her bells and bells silent above the singing grass,
В В В В Still the old marigold her light sprinkles like riches to the poor.
В В В В Snapdragon still his changeling blossom shakes with the burden of the bees,
В В В В And the strong bindweed creeps and winds and springs on high a conqueror.

В В В В *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *

В В В В Would now her eyes grieve to behold snapdragon, foxglove, marigold
В В В В Daily diminish in their sweet and bindweed wreathing over all--
В В В В Weed and grass and weed and grass, friendless, melancholy, cold,
В В В В Wreathing the earth like wreathing snow from bare wall to low greening wall?

В В В В Old were her eyes that lingered on old trees and grass and flowers trim.
В В В В She smelt the ripe pears when they drooped and fell and broke upon the path.
В В В В Old were her thoughts of things of old; her present thoughts were few and dim;
В В В В Her eyes saw not the things she saw; she listened, to no living breath.

В В В В Her youth and prime and autumn time bloomed in her thought all light and sweet:
В В В В No wallflower more of sweet could hold, of sunny light no marigold.
В В В В Fruit on her mind's boughs ripened full, in summer's and calm autumn's heat:
В В В В Then fell, for there came none to pick; but winter came, and she was old.

В В В В Now if her sons come they will find--not her: her empty garden only,
В В В В The wallflower done and snapdragon still swinging with the greedy bees,
В В В В Marigold glittering in the grass, scant foxglove ringing faintly, lonely,
В В В В Close red fruit beading the long boughs and bindweed wreathing where it please.

В В В В A tawny lean cat Marmalade slinks like a panther through the tall
В В В В Thin bending grass and watches long a scholar thrush rehearsing song;
В В В В Or children running in the sun hunt and hunt a well lost ball;
В В В В But most the garden sleeps away the day, but still, when eves are long,

В В В В When eves are long and no moon rises, and nervous, still, is all the air,
В В В В That small stiff figure moves again, silent amid the hushing grass;
В В В В In the firm-carven lime tree's shade she moves, and meets her old thoughts there,
В В В В Then in the deepening dark is lost, or her light steps unnoted pass.

В В В В Only that careless garden keeps secure her memory though it sleeps,
В В В В And the bright flowers and tyrant weed and tall grass shaking its loud seed
В В В В Less lovely were if wanting her who like a living thought still creeps
В В В В And sees what once she saw and music hears of her living sons and dead.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, John Frederick Freeman delivers a powerful performance in "Merrill's Garden"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
